title: Polyphlebium angustatum
taxonomy: Polyphlebium angustatum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Polyphlebium, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a family of filmy ferns, which are known for their delicate and translucent fronds. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns. Polyphlebium angustatum is a unique species within this classification, characterized by its specific frond shape and size.

Research on Polyphlebium angustatum suggests that it may be used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and fever.
